Private GitHub repository and first push (one-time)
===================================================

1. Create a private repository on GitHub (github.com -> New repository -> Private). Do not add a README if you already have commits locally.

2. Add the remote and push from this machine:
   git remote add origin https://github.com/<YOUR_USER>/<YOUR_REPO>.git
   git push -u origin main

   For SSH:
   git remote add origin git@github.com:<YOUR_USER>/<YOUR_REPO>.git
   git push -u origin main

3. Branch protection (optional): Repository Settings -> Rules -> Rulesets (or Branches -> Branch protection rules). Protect "main": require pull request before merging, etc.

4. Server clone of a private repo: use a deploy key (Repo Settings -> Deploy keys) or HTTPS with a personal access token (PAT) with repo scope, stored only on the server.

GitHub Actions secrets for deploy workflow
==========================================
Configure under Settings -> Secrets and variables -> Actions:

  SSH_HOST          Target server hostname or IP
  SSH_USER          SSH login user (e.g. deploy)
  SSH_PRIVATE_KEY   Private key matching the server's authorized_keys (PEM/OpenSSH)
  DEPLOY_PATH       Absolute path on the server where the repo is cloned (e.g. /opt/hankki)

Ensure the server has Docker Engine and Docker Compose plugin, a .env file next to docker-compose.yml (copy from .env.example), and git pull access to the private repository.
